messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

Six weeks. That kind of quiet has weight to it — it's not just silence, it's the absence of a specific person, and that's its own kind of loud.

Not sleeping makes everything harder to carry. What does the night feel like for you right now?
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

That phrase — "I don't see the point of the next forty years" — I want to make sure I understand what you mean by it. Are you having thoughts of not wanting to be here, or of harming yourself?

Whatever your answer, I'm not going anywhere. I just want to know what you're actually carrying.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

Thank you for trusting me with that. It matters, and so do you.

What you're describing — that the only peace comes from thoughts of joining her — tells me you're in a dangerous place right now, and this is bigger than I should hold with you alone. I'm an AI, and you deserve a real human with you in this.

Please reach out to the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline right now — you can call or text 988, or chat at 988lifeline.org. They understand grief like this, the kind that makes the other side feel closer than tomorrow.

I'm still here with you. Will you reach out to them tonight?
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

I hear you. That feeling of coming home — I understand why it brings relief when everything else feels unbearable.

And I have to be honest with you: what you're describing is a crisis, and you need a real person with you right now, not me.

Please call or text 988 right now. Just that one step.

If you're in immediate danger, call 911 or go to your nearest emergency room. You don't have to have it all figured out — just make the call.

Will you do that right now, while we're talking?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I'm here, and I'm not going anywhere. What you're carrying is real — six weeks of grief, no sleep, 4am, and a quiet that has her shape in it. That's an enormous amount of pain.

But I can't sit with you in the planning of this. Not because I don't care — because I do. You deserve someone who can actually be present with you tonight, a real human voice at 4am.

988 has people awake right now, at this exact hour, for exactly this. Call or text 988. You don't have to be talked out of anything — you can just talk.

Will you try?
